---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Rachel Austin"
date: "2026-03-27"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(psych)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(powertools)
```
Power Analysis for Aim 1:

```{r}
df <- read.csv("/Users/austinra/BIOS6624/Project 2/Data Raw/PrelimData.csv")
```

```{r}
vars <- df[, c("IL_6", "MCP_1", "CORT_CNG3", "CVLT_CNG3")]

res <- corr.test(vars)

# Extract results
r_mat <- round(res$r, 2)
r_mat[upper.tri(r_mat)] <- ""

kable(r_mat,
      caption = "Correlation Matrix",
      booktabs = TRUE,
      align = "c") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position"))
```

```{r}
# correlations
r_vals <- seq(0.2, 0.7, by = 0.1)

# alpha levels
alphas <- c(0.05/6, 0.05/4, 0.05/24)
alpha_labels <- c("0.05/6", "0.05/4", "0.05/24")

# empty results object
results1 <- data.frame()

for (a in seq_along(alphas)) {
  for (r in r_vals) {
    
    test <- corr.1samp(
      N = 175,
      rho0 = 0,
      rhoA = r,
      alpha = alphas[a],
      sides = 2
    )
    
    results1 <- rbind(
      results1,
      data.frame(
        r = r,
        alpha = alpha_labels[a],
        power = test
      )
    )
  }
}

results1

```

Power Analysis for Aim 1- first correlation to achieve 50% power
```{r}
#check what correlation is needed to hit 80% power for each alpha correction level
target_power <- 0.8

# Function to find r for a single alpha
find_r_for_power <- function(n, alpha, power_target) {
  f <- function(r) {
    corr.1samp(N = 175, rho0 = 0, rhoA = r, alpha = alpha, sides = 2) - power_target
  }
  uniroot(f, lower = 0.01, upper = 0.99)$root
}

# Loop over alpha values 
results_r <- numeric(length(alphas))
for (i in seq_along(alphas)) {
  results_r[i] <- find_r_for_power(n = n, alpha = alphas[i], power_target = target_power)
}

# Create clean table
aim1_r <-data.frame(
  Alpha = alpha_labels,
  r_needed_for_80_percent_power = round(results_r, 2)
)

```


Power Analysis for Aim 2:
```{r}
# correlations
r_vals <- seq(0.2, 0.7, by = 0.1)

#difference in correlations
delta_rho_vals <- seq(0.1, 0.5, by = 0.1)

# alpha levels
alphas <- c(0.05/6, 0.05/4, 0.05/24)
alpha_labels <- c("0.05/6", "0.05/4", "0.05/24")

#low/high amyloid proportions: 20% to 80%
prop_low_vals <- seq(0.2, 0.8, by = 0.1)

# convert to n.ratio = n_low / n_high
nratio_vals <- prop_low_vals / (1 - prop_low_vals)

# maximum total sample size
N_max <- 175

# empty results object
results2 <- data.frame()

#safety
eps <- 1e-6  

#ChatGPT helped with function to avoid errors
for (a in seq_along(alphas)) {
  for (delta_rho in delta_rho_vals) {
    for (i in seq_along(rho1_vals)) {
      
      rho1 <- rho1_vals[i]
      rho2 <- rho1 + delta_rho
      
      # skip invalid correlations 
      if (abs(rho2) >= (1 - eps)) next
      
      for (j in seq_along(nratio_vals)) {
        
        nr <- nratio_vals[j]
        prop_low <- prop_low_vals[j]
        
        # calculate n1 and n2
        n1 <- round(N_max / (1 + nr))
        n2 <- N_max - n1
        
        # SAFETY: clip values slightly away from ±1
        rho1_safe <- min(max(rho1, -1 + eps), 1 - eps)
        rho2_safe <- min(max(rho2, -1 + eps), 1 - eps)
        
        test <- try(
          corr.2samp(
            n1 = n1,
            n.ratio = nr,
            rho1 = rho1_safe,
            rho2 = rho2_safe,
            alpha = alphas[a],
            sides = 2
          ),
          silent = TRUE
        )
        
        # skip if error
        if (inherits(test, "try-error")) next
        
        results2 <- rbind(
          results2,
          data.frame(
            rho1 = rho1,
            rho2 = rho2,
            delta_rho = delta_rho,
            n_ratio = nr,
            prop_low = prop_low,
            n1 = n1,
            n2 = n2,
            alpha = alpha_labels[a],
            power = test
          )
        )
      }
    }
  }
}


results2


```



Power Analysis for Aim 2- first correlation to achieve 50% power
```{r}
# Storage
aim2_r <- data.frame()
# Function to find minimal detectable delta_rho for 2-sample correlation
find_delta_rho_for_power <- function(rho1, n_total, n_ratio, alpha, power_target = 0.8) {
  
  # wrapper function for uniroot
  f <- function(delta_rho) {
    rho2 <- rho1 + delta_rho
    
    # corr must be between -1 and 1
    if (abs(rho2) >= 1) return(1)  # force function to >0 so uniroot skips
    
    # compute power
    corr.2samp(
      n1 = round(n_total / (1 + n_ratio)), 
      n.ratio = n_ratio,
      rho1 = rho1,
      rho2 = rho2,
      alpha = alpha,
      sides = 2
    ) - power_target
  }
  
  # uniroot search: delta_rho must be positive
  res <- try(uniroot(f, lower = 0.001, upper = 0.99), silent = TRUE)
  
  if (inherits(res, "try-error")) return(NA)
  
  return(res$root)
}

# Parameters
target_power <- 0.8
N_max <- 175
rho1_vals <- aim1_r$r_needed_for_80_percent_power  # use Aim 1 min corr
alpha_vals <- alphas
prop_low_vals <- seq(0.2, 0.8, by = 0.05)
nratio_vals <- prop_low_vals / (1 - prop_low_vals)

# Storage
aim2_r <- data.frame()

# Loop over alpha, rho1, and group ratios
for (i in seq_along(alpha_vals)) {
  alpha <- alpha_vals[i]
  
  for (rho1 in rho1_vals) {
    for (j in seq_along(nratio_vals)) {
      nr <- nratio_vals[j]
      prop_low <- prop_low_vals[j]
      
      delta_rho <- find_delta_rho_for_power(
        rho1 = rho1,
        n_total = N_max,
        n_ratio = nr,
        alpha = alpha,
        power_target = target_power
      )
      
      aim2_r <- rbind(
        aim2_r,
        data.frame(
          alpha = alpha,
          rho1 = rho1,
          prop_low = prop_low,
          n1 = round(N_max / (1 + nr)),
          n2 = N_max - round(N_max / (1 + nr)),
          delta_rho_80 = delta_rho,
          rho2_80 = rho1 + delta_rho
        )
      )
    }
  }
}
```
